title: Multi-thread depth on large deals doubled this quarter
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts versus 2.1 contacts in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts versus 2.1 contacts in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: The multi-threading improvement on $250K+ deals shows direct correlation with win rate improvement, suggesting better buying committee engagement.

title: Champion re-engagement drives stage advancement within three weeks
anchor: Nine of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement events in April.
connect: Champion documentation rate crossed 50% for the first time since tracking began.
body: Nine of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement events in April. Champion documentation rate crossed 50% for the first time since tracking began.
trace: Champion re-engagement events in April triggered stage movement in 64% of cases, with documentation discipline improving.

title: Sterling deal expanded buying committee post-executive demo
anchor: Executive demo on April 18 grew Sterling's engaged contacts from 4 to 7, adding CFO and two business-unit leads.
connect: Committees that grow during evaluation historically close at 26% above flat committees.
body: Executive demo on April 18 grew Sterling's engaged contacts from 4 to 7, adding CFO and two business-unit leads. Committees that grow during evaluation historically close at 26% above flat committees.
trace: The Sterling deal shows buying committee expansion pattern that correlates with higher close rates based on historical data.

title: Enterprise proposal cycles compressed by 11 days this quarter
anchor: Median proposal-to-close time runs at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1.
connect: Q2 enterprise closes with 5+ thread depth account for 7 of 9 total closes.
body: Median proposal-to-close time runs at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1. Q2 enterprise closes with 5+ thread depth account for 7 of 9 total closes.
trace: Proposal stage compression aligns with increased multi-threading on enterprise deals.

title: Q3 pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x quota
anchor: Open Q3 pipeline of $4.2M covers the $1.24M aggregate quota with 79% showing live engagement in the last 14 days.
connect: Single-threaded coverage share dropped to 22% in Q2 from 34% in Q1.
body: Open Q3 pipeline of $4.2M covers the $1.24M aggregate quota with 79% showing live engagement in the last 14 days. Single-threaded coverage share dropped to 22% in Q2 from 34% in Q1.
trace: Coverage ratio with engagement quality and reduced single-threading suggests healthier pipeline composition.

title: Aged proposal deals advance within days of re-issued proposals
anchor: Eleven of 14 proposal-stage deals aged over 21 days advanced or closed in the last 30 days.
connect: Six of those 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
body: Eleven of 14 proposal-stage deals aged over 21 days advanced or closed in the last 30 days. Six of those 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
trace: The correlation between proposal re-issuance and advancement suggests procurement timing rather than deal quality issues.

title: Mid-market win rate jumped 8 points with ICP alignment driving the lift
anchor: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% versus 19.1% in Q1, with 19 wins from 70 opportunities.
connect: ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
body: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% versus 19.1% in Q1, with 19 wins from 70 opportunities. ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
trace: The win rate improvement concentrates heavily in ICP-aligned opportunities, suggesting targeting discipline is paying off.

title: Named champions correlate with 17-point win rate advantage
anchor: Q2 deals with named Champion field show 38% win rate versus 21% for deals without champion documentation.
connect: Champion field completion rate crossed 50% in Q2 for the first time since tracking began.
body: Q2 deals with named Champion field show 38% win rate versus 21% for deals without champion documentation. Champion field completion rate crossed 50% in Q2 for the first time since tracking began.
trace: The correlation between champion documentation and win rates aligns with improved documentation discipline.

title: Outbound sequence revision drives 5-point conversion lift
anchor: Revised outbound cadence launched April 5th converts 42 of 300 contacts to discovery meetings at 14%.
connect: Prior sequence cadence ran at 9% conversion to meetings.
body: Revised outbound cadence launched April 5th converts 42 of 300 contacts to discovery meetings at 14%. Prior sequence cadence ran at 9% conversion to meetings.
trace: The sequence revision shows immediate improvement in contact-to-meeting conversion rates.

title: Beacon head-to-head win rate climbs to 61% after four-quarter slump
anchor: Q2 Beacon Systems matchups show 11-7 record for 61.1% win rate.
connect: Trailing four quarters Beacon win rate averaged 24%.
body: Q2 Beacon Systems matchups show 11-7 record for 61.1% win rate. Trailing four quarters Beacon win rate averaged 24%.
trace: The Beacon head-to-head improvement represents a significant competitive turnaround from the trailing four-quarter pattern.

title: Beacon competitive frequency increases as win rate improves
anchor: Beacon appeared in 18 of 62 competitive opportunities in Q2, up from 11 of 58 in Q1.
connect: Frequency climbed from 19% to 29% of competitive deals over the same window.
body: Beacon appeared in 18 of 62 competitive opportunities in Q2, up from 11 of 58 in Q1. Frequency climbed from 19% to 29% of competitive deals over the same window.
trace: Beacon's increased competitive presence coincides with Atlas's improved win rate against them.

title: Meridian AI competitive presence more than doubles post-Series B
anchor: Meridian AI appeared in 12 of 47 active Q2 deals at 26% frequency versus 10% in Q1.
connect: Seven of 12 Meridian deals reached negotiation within 30 days of their March 14th Series B announcement.
body: Meridian AI appeared in 12 of 47 active Q2 deals at 26% frequency versus 10% in Q1. Seven of 12 Meridian deals reached negotiation within 30 days of their March 14th Series B announcement.
trace: Meridian's funding announcement appears to correlate with both increased competitive presence and deal velocity.

title: Beacon battlecard utilization jumps 41 points with win rate correlation
anchor: Battlecard usage in active Beacon head-to-head deals climbed to 72% in April from 31% in February.
connect: Head-to-head win rate versus Beacon improved 14 points over the same utilization window.
body: Battlecard usage in active Beacon head-to-head deals climbed to 72% in April from 31% in February. Head-to-head win rate versus Beacon improved 14 points over the same utilization window.
trace: The timing alignment between battlecard adoption and win rate improvement suggests content effectiveness.

title: Customer success flags surface $310K expansion potential from Q2 onboards
anchor: Five of 9 Q2 onboarding completions flagged as expansion-ready with $310K combined ARR potential.
connect: Three of the 5 flagged accounts already have logged discovery touches averaging 2.1 contacts each.
body: Five of 9 Q2 onboarding completions flagged as expansion-ready with $310K combined ARR potential. Three of the 5 flagged accounts already have logged discovery touches averaging 2.1 contacts each.
trace: The customer success to sales handoff on expansion opportunities shows early engagement on 60% of flagged accounts.

title: Outcome reason field drives Q2 capture completeness above 85%
anchor: Nineteen of 23 full activity captures on Q2 closed deals landed after the April 5th outcome reason field launch.
connect: Full capture rate sits at 88% in Q2 versus 64% in Q1.
body: Nineteen of 23 full activity captures on Q2 closed deals landed after the April 5th outcome reason field launch. Full capture rate sits at 88% in Q2 versus 64% in Q1.
trace: The outcome reason field launch correlates directly with improved CRM data quality and capture discipline.

title: Inbound conversion maintains 31% while outbound sequence improves
anchor: Inbound-to-meeting conversion holds at 31% over the April window.
connect: Revised outbound sequence converts at 14% over the same period.
body: Inbound-to-meeting conversion holds at 31% over the April window. Revised outbound sequence converts at 14% over the same period.
trace: The inbound conversion baseline provides context for the outbound sequence improvement performance.
